# instructions.md

> 📖 First time? Read the [Instructions Guide](../docs/instructions-guide.md) for setup and placement details.

---

## Feature Summary

[1–2 paragraphs: What are you building? What problem does it solve? What does it replace or improve?]

---

## Business Context

- **Why This Matters:** [Strategic importance — compliance deadlines, leadership mandates, cost savings, competitive pressure. Why now?]
- **Success Looks Like:** [Measurable outcomes — e.g., "reduce remediation time from 12 days to under 48 hours."]

---

## User Context

- **Target Users:** [Who uses this feature? Can be broad (e.g., "all developers in the organization") or specific (e.g., "DevOps engineers managing CI/CD pipelines").]
- **Current Pain Points:** [What problems do users face today? Be as specific as you can — hours wasted, error rates, workarounds.]
- **User Goals:** [What do users want to achieve with this feature?]

---

## Scope

- **In Scope:** [What this feature WILL do — list the boundaries explicitly]
- **Out of Scope:** [What this feature will NOT do — prevents scope creep and sets expectations]

---

## Team Context *(Optional)*

> Helps tailor the spec for the right audience and identify reviewers early.

- **Engineering Teams Involved:** [Team names and their responsibilities for this feature]
- **Key Stakeholders:** [Product, Engineering, Security, or other leads who should review or provide input]

---

## Constraints and Compliance *(Optional)*

> Include if your feature has regulatory, security, accessibility, or data handling requirements. The AI cannot infer these from the feature description alone.

- **Compliance:** [e.g., HIPAA, SOC 2, FedRAMP, GDPR, SOX]
- **Accessibility:** [e.g., WCAG 2.1 AA, screen reader support required]
- **Security:** [e.g., data encryption at rest, SSO required, no PII in logs]
- **Data Residency:** [e.g., EU user data must stay in EU regions]
- **Other:** [Any hard constraints — performance SLAs, platform support, backward compatibility]

---

## Reference Materials *(Optional)*

> The PM Lead agent reads these materials and uses them as additional context when generating your spec. Local files in your workspace are the most reliable. URLs may work but depend on access — the PM Lead agent will confirm whether it can read linked content before proceeding.

- **Related Specs:** [File paths or links to existing specifications]
- **Architecture Docs:** [File paths or links to design documents, ADRs, or diagrams]
- **Product Requirements:** [File paths or links to PRDs, epics, or user stories]

---

## Technical Context *(Optional)*

> Most PMs won't have this upfront — that's fine. This is typically defined by the engineering team. Include what you know; skip what you don't.

- **Component Affected:** [Which system, service, or component does this feature change or create?]
- **Technology Stack:** [Languages, frameworks, runtimes — e.g., TypeScript, Node.js, Python]
- **Integration Points:** [External APIs, services, or systems this feature connects to]
- **Architecture:** [Microservices, monolith, serverless? Where does this feature fit?]

---

## Writing Style *(Optional)*

> Want the generated spec to sound like you wrote it? Provide 1–2 documents that represent your writing style. The agent will match your voice, tone, and structure patterns while following all template and quality standards.
>
> **Recommended formats:** .md, .txt, .rtf — for best results, convert your samples to .txt before referencing them here. Formats like .docx and .pdf may work but can introduce conversion overhead and occasional failures.

- [File path to writing sample 1]
- [File path to writing sample 2 *(optional)*]
